title: Winter Provisioning of a Polar Research Base Supply Tree
strategies: trivial: top off every station to safety factor 4 (evaluator baseline; always feasible for both floors) -> ~0.1

greedy: single-Lagrangian water-filling for the programme fill floor only, ignoring per-station life-support local floors; cheap+feasible on loose/no-critical instances but violates a local floor on tight-critical instances -> 0 there

strong: two-stage design -- pin each life-support station to its local floor k_c^min=Loss^{-1}((1-alpha)mu_c/sd_c), then water-fill the residual network deficit over the rest; feasible everywhere at near-min cost
invalid: zero safety stock everywhere -> programme fill collapses below beta and every local floor is violated -> infeasible on every instance -> 0
